data.title: Orthodox Synagogue in Orlando, FL Sues for Religious Discrimination
data.description:
Orlando, FL, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The Orlando Torah Center (OTC), an Orthodox synagogue in Orlando’s Sand Lake Hills neighborhood, filed a federal lawsuit today against Orange County and its Board of County Commissioners after they unfairly denied the synagogue’s application to expand. 

According to OTC’s counsel, this case is about ensuring that minority faith institutions are not held to a different standard and that religious freedom is fully protected under the law.  The Orlando Torah Center’s lawsuit seeks to defend its rights under the First Amendment, the federal Religious Land Use and Institutionalized Persons Act (RLUIPA), and Florida’s Religious Freedom Restoration Act. 

“Religious freedom is not optional; it is a constitutional guarantee,” said counsel Roman Storzer of Storzer and Associates, a leader in First Amendment and RLUIPA law. “OTC has every legal and moral right to exist, expand, and serve its community without undue burden or discrimination.” 

The Orlando Torah Center, located along South Apopka Vineland Road in an area locally known as 'Church Row' for its concentration of more than a dozen churches, has served a walkable congregation since 2015. While numerous churches, a public school, and a daycare center have all received similar zoning approvals in similar zoning districts, OTC’s request to expand for its religious use faced intense public opposition, including antisemitic remarks during public hearings, and was ultimately denied by the Board of County Commissioners on July 1, 2025, according to the legal complaint. 

“Individuals of all faiths have a right to worship without outside interference,” said Mr. Storzer. “OTC members have been part of their neighborhood for more than a decade. They have operated peacefully and built lasting relationships with families of all faiths. Disappointedly, they were denied the same rights routinely granted to others.” 

OTC seeks to expand its facility to accommodate increased attendance, children’s programs, and enhanced safety measures—especially important given the rise in antisemitic attacks targeting Jewish institutions across the country. According to the lawsuit, OTC members face severe hardship due to lack of space, including families having to participate in religious activities outdoors in the Florida heat, children being unable to attend holiday programs, and attendees being exposed to safety risks due to the inability to secure the building. 

The case was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Florida, Case No. 6:25-cv-014.

To read the full complaint, please click here.
For media inquiries, please contact OrlandoTorahCenter@redbanyan.com. 


About Orlando Torah Center
Founded in 2011, the Orlando Torah Center (OTC) is an Orthodox synagogue committed to fostering a vibrant, welcoming community rooted in Torah learning, prayer, and acts of kindness. Serving a diverse and growing congregation, OTC offers educational programs, youth activities, and spiritual guidance for individuals and families at every stage of observance. As part of Orlando’s thriving Torah infrastructure, OTC plays a central role in supporting Jewish life and continuity in the region.


Contact:
OrlandoTorahCenter@redbanyan.com

data.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Investigation of Albany International Corp. (AIN) Announced by Holzer & Holzer, LLC
data.description:
ATLANTA, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Holzer & Holzer, LLC is investigating whether Albany International Corp. (“Albany International” or the “Company”) (NYSE: AIN) complied with federal securities laws. On July 31, 2025, Albany International reported operating results for its second quarter 2025, noting its “performance was largely impacted by certain timing and operational issues[.]” Following this news, the price of the Company’s stock dropped. 

If you purchased Albany International stock and suffered a loss on that investment, you are encouraged to contact Corey Holzer, Esq. at cholzer@holzerlaw.com or Joshua Karr, Esq. at jkarr@holzerlaw.com, call our toll-free number at (888) 508-6832, or visit our website at www.holzerlaw.com/case/albany-international/ to discuss your legal rights.

Holzer & Holzer, LLC, an ISS top rated securities litigation law firm for 2021, 2022, and 2023, dedicates its practice to vigorous representation of shareholders and investors in litigation nationwide, including shareholder class action and derivative litigation. Since its founding in 2000, Holzer & Holzer attorneys have played critical roles in recovering hundreds of millions of dollars for shareholders victimized by fraud and other corporate misconduct. More information about the firm is available through its website, www.holzerlaw.com, and upon request from the firm. Holzer & Holzer, LLC has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Corey Holzer is the attorney responsible for its content.

CONTACT:

(888) 508-6832 (toll-free)

data.title: Saint-Gobain Earnings, Sales Narrowly Miss Expectations
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud 




  Saint-Gobain reported earnings and sales for the first half that were slightly below analysts' expectations, but reiterated its full-year outlook and said that it has no direct exposure to customs tariffs. 



  Net profit for the half year fell 1.9% to 1.63 billion euros ($1.86 billion), the French construction-materials company. The decline was mainly driven by higher tax and interest expenses. 



  Sales rose 1.7% to 23.85 billion euros. Meanwhile, operating profit was 1.9% higher at 2.80 billion euros, with a margin that increased to 11.8% from 11.7% a year before. 



  Analysts had forecast Saint-Gobain to report sales of 23.99 billion euros, operating profit of 2.83 billion euros and net profit of 1.65 billion euros, according to consensus estimates provided by Visible Alpha. 



  "Asia and emerging countries continued to drive growth for the group and Europe reported a further sequential improvement, while North America saw a slight decrease in sales," Chairman and Chief Executive Benoit Bazin said. 



  For the year as a whole, the company confirmed its expectations of an operating margin of more than 11%. 





  Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 31, 2025 13:03 ET (17:03 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Introducing Rippling Travel: For Faster Bookings, Smarter Spending, and Better Travel
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO, July 31, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Rippling, a leading business software company, today announced the launch of Rippling Travel, allowing companies to save more time and money on business travel. Rippling Travel unifies expense management, corporate cards, bill pay, payroll, and travel booking all within a single, integrated system – making it faster, easier, and cheaper than ever to book and manage corporate travel.

“At Rippling, our focus has always been to free people to work on hard problems by automating away repetitive admin work so they can focus on strategic work,” said Matt Epstein, General Manager, Rippling Spend.

That's why we launched Rippling Travel. It makes it insanely easy for employees to book flights, hotels, and cars without having to worry about what is (and isn't) in-policy. And for companies to automatically control every penny their employees spend on business travel, from in-flight seat upgrades to reimbursements on the road.

Corporate travel has historically been a headache. For employees, it takes time and causes stress. For employers, it requires endless administrative tasks and back-and-forths on approvals. In fact, in a recent Rippling survey, 80% of companies said business travel is critical to revenue generation – but 93% still using legacy travel platforms report budget overruns and administrative inefficiencies.

Rippling Travel solves these issues, providing an alternative to outdated software and disparate systems with a single system for unified travel and expense management that offers:

  • Seamless and cost conscious booking: Rippling Travel makes it quick and easy for employees to book and manage their travel. We partnered with Duffel to offer options from Expedia Group, Priceline, Booking.com, and direct airline integrations – all from within one intuitive platform.
  • Hyper-custom policies: Rippling Travel allows you to build hyper-custom policies around flights, hotels, and car rentals in just a few clicks. Rippling Travel’s deep integration with other business systems means policies automatically adapt to internal organizational changes (such as promotions or procedural updates).
  • Comprehensive reporting and analytics: Integrated into the Spend suite, Rippling Travel empowers employers with real-time, comprehensive data and analytics reports on all travel and spend related matters across the business.

Ready to consolidate all spend management with Rippling – including Travel? Learn more and sign up for a demo today.

About Rippling
Rippling gives businesses one place to run HR, IT, and Spend – globally. It brings together all of the workforce systems that are normally scattered across a company, like payroll, expenses, benefits, and computers. For the first time ever, you can manage and automate every part of the employee lifecycle in a single system. Based in San Francisco, CA, Rippling has raised $1.8B from the world's top investors – including Kleiner Perkins, Founders Fund, Sequoia, and Bedrock.


Bobby Whithorne 
bobby@rippling.com

data.title: Nymex Overview: Oil Drops $1 After Three-Day Rally; Tariffs, Russia in Focus — OPIS
data.description:
Crude contracts fell by around $1 heading into Thursday afternoon trade as investors took profits following a three-day gain amid global economic optimism, while the market continued to closely monitor the latest developments on trade talk and possible additional U.S. sanctions on Russian oil.

At 11:45 a.m. ET, September West Texas Intermediate was down $1.35 at around $68.65/bbl and October WTI was also $1.35 lower to $67.60/bbl.

London-based September ICE Brent was 80cts lower at $72.45/bbl and October Brent was off by $1.35 at about $71.15/bbl.

Both oil benchmarks surged $3-4/bbl in the past three sessions after Washington reached preliminary trade deals with the European Union and South Korea.

Meanwhile, oil supply worries increased after President Trump said on Tuesday that Russia could face tougher tariffs if Kremlin did not meet Trump's 10-day deadline reach a ceasefire in Ukraine.

Refined product contracts also tracked oil's weakness. More-active September RBOB was 3.85cts lower at $2.1635/gal and August RBOB was off by 3.65cts at $2.2325/gal. September ULSD was 2.10cts lower at $2.3895/gal and August ULSD was down by 2.55cts at $2.393/gal.

In refinery news, the head of refining at PBF Energy said Thursday he expects the company's fire-stricken 157,000 b/d Martinez refinery in Northern California to fully restart by the end of 2025, which was later than PBF's previous target of end-September.

In addition, PBF's hydrocracker turnaround at its 166,200 b/d Torrance, Calif., refinery is expected to be complete by the beginning of September.

In the spot market, most products mirrored the movements in NYMEX futures without any notable changes. 




  This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 





  --Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 31, 2025 12:40 ET (16:40 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ICE Canola Weaker With Fund Selling
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market was weaker at midday Thursday, hitting fresh two-week lows as losses in Chicago soyoil spilled over to weigh on values.

Speculative funds were likely behind much of the selling as they adjusted positions, said a trader, adding that the relatively small open interest in the canola market compared to the daily volumes was leading to large day-to-day price swings with a move off the session lows likely before the close.

Tightening old crop supplies and wide crush margins remained supportive from a fundamental standpoint.

Mixed Prairie growing conditions kept some weather premiums in the market, with dryness in some areas countered by relatively favorable weather elsewhere.

An estimated 22,700 canola contracts traded as of 10:54 a.m. CDT. 




  Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton at 10:54 a.m. CDT:

data.title: Figma's IPO is a 'bellwether event.' Here's why investors want in.
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi and Mike Murphy

Design-software company's market cap tips the scales at more than $19 billion, as the IPO priced at $33 a share to raise more than $1.2 billion for the company and shareholders

The initial public offering from design-software company Figma Inc. was set to at least double in its opening trade Thursday in what one analyst described as a "bellwether event for the tech sector."

That analyst, Derek Hernandez of PitchBook Data, also said Figma (FIG) stands out from other companies that have gone public recently as a "generational software-as-a-service company that has achieved a near-monopolistic hold on the product design market."

The bid-ask spread for Figma rose to $90 to $95 a share as of midday Thursday, up from $65 to $70 earlier in the day, according to FactSet data.

That suggests that the stock could roughly triple its initial public offering price of $33 a share, or at least double it.

Figma has "systematically displaced" larger rivals such as Adobe Inc.'s (ADBE) Adobe XD product and the privately held Sketch BV, Hernandez said.

Adobe tried to buy its fledgling rival for about $20 billion several years ago, but the deal was called off due to concerns about regulatory scrutiny.

The pricing of Figma's IPO at $33 a share topped the projected range of $30 to $32 a share, which had been raised earlier this week from an original range of $25 to $28.

At the IPO price, Figma would have a market capitalization of $19.3 billion, based on 585 million shares outstanding on a fully diluted basis, which includes unvested stock options.

A total of 36.94 million shares were sold in the IPO, raising $1.22 billion. That included the company selling 12.47 million shares to raise $411.7 million, and shareholders selling 24.46 million shares to raise $807.3 million.

Trading of the stock on the New York Stock Exchange is expected to begin later Thursday.

Figma offers investors a "strong and consistent growth rate for a long time" due partly to its international presence and potential domestic expansion, Hernandez said.

He said he wasn't surprised that the underwriters of the deal raised Figma's estimated price range, given the company's high profile as a private software company that had been eyeing an IPO for years before market conditions improved enough in 2025 to move ahead.

"I don't think we've seen a company as good as this for a while," Hernandez told MarketWatch. "It's a very unique opportunity. It would have gone public a while ago in another market."

Read more: Figma files for an IPO. What investors should know about the Adobe design rival.

Experts say Figma's IPO - assuming a positive debut - will likely persuade other private software companies to go public and help re-energize the market. That group could include AI customer-service provider Genesys Cloud Services Inc., graphic-design-software maker Canva Pty Ltd. and data-analytics company Databricks Inc.

"Investors have been starved of new entrants into public markets in recent years," DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria said in a Monday note. "With elongated timelines between capital raises and a liquidity event, these organizations have gotten fit."

Hernandez said the $20 billion valuation by Adobe was like a "line in the sand" for Figma's IPO pricing, so he wasn't surprised when the price target for the IPO was raised on Monday.

With more than 1,600 employees and 13 million monthly active users, Figma products were being used by 95% of the Fortune 500 as of March, with more than half of its revenue coming from outside the U.S.

Dylan Field, co-founder and chief executive of the company, won a Thiel Fellowship to pursue entrepreneurial projects early in his career.

Field is selling 2.35 million shares in the IPO, for proceeds of $77.6 million.

Mamoon Hamid, a board member at Figma and a managing member at venture-capital firm Kleiner Perkins, is selling up to 2.76 million shares, valued at about $91 million.

Other principal shareholders of Figma include affiliates of Index Ventures, Greylock Partners and Sequoia Capital.

The IPO from Figma comes after several successful larger IPOs this year, including AI data-center specialist CoreWeave Inc. CRWV, payments-technology company Circle Internet Group Inc. (CRCL), online brokerage eToro Group Ltd. (ETOR) and online banking-services company Chime Financial Inc. (CHYM).

More: Ambiq's stock soars after IPO. Here's where the company sees its place in the AI boom.

-Steve Gelsi -Mike Murphy

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  07-31-25 1229ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Nordic Stocks Decline; Lindab International Falls Furthest
data.description:
This article was automatically generated by Dow Jones using technology from Automated Insights.

Stocks in the Nordic region declined Thursday, with the OMX Nordic 40 Index declining 1.5%.

Lindab International AB posted the largest decline among large stocks during the session, dropping 5.4%, followed by DSV A/S shares, which dropped 4.7%. Shares of RaySearch Laboratories AB Series B declined 4.7%.

Aker ASA was the biggest leader, gaining 8.9%, and Securitas AB Series B increased 3.1%. BioArctic AB Series B rounded out the top three leaders on Thursday, with shares increasing 2.9%.

In Denmark, the OMX Copenhagen 20 Index declined 2.2%, and the OMX Helsinki 25 Index, which tracks Finland's most-traded stocks, fell 0.6%.

Stocks on Sweden's OMX Stockholm 30 Index dropped 1.0%, while Norway's Oslo Exchange Benchmark Index_GI rose 0.2%. Shares in Iceland decreased, with the OMX Iceland All-Share PI Equity Index declining 0.2%.

Elsewhere in Europe, indexes were mixed, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index dropping 0.8% and the FTSE 100 Index flat from the previous close.

In commodities news, Brent crude oil futures were down 1.8%, and gold futures were down 0.1%. Bitcoin was up 1.2% to $118,289.

On the currency front, the WSJ Dollar Index rose 0.1% to 96.72.

Against the euro, the Danish krone was flat, the Swedish krona was flat, the Icelandic krona was down 0.1%, the Norwegian krone was down 0.1%, and the U.S. dollar was down 0.2%.

Against the U.S. dollar, the Danish krone was up 0.2%, the euro was up 0.2%, the Swedish krona was up 0.2%, the Icelandic krona was up 0.1%, and the Norwegian krone was up 0.1%.

Data source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  July 31, 2025 12:21 ET (16:21 GMT)

data.title: People are spending less on their teeth, and this stock is plunging
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

Align Technology's stock plunged, as people are choosing lower-cost metal options over its Invisalign offering for teeth straightening, amid worries about the economy

Align Technology Inc.'s stock was having its worst day in more than 20 years on Thursday, after the Invisalign teeth-straightening treatment company missed analysts' profit estimates and said it expects to lay off employees, as consumers cut back spending on dental procedures due to economic uncertainty.

"Recent dental industry surveys for the second quarter suggest there was less overall patient traffic, fewer orthodontic case starts and patient hesitation toward electric procedures," Chief Executive Joe Hogan said on the post-earnings call with analysts, according to an AlphaSense transcript.

He said the reason for the hesitation has to do with uncertainties surrounding tariffs and how they will affect the economy, as well as less affordable financing options for orthodontic treatment.

"It's patients being concerned in a sense of, can they afford that kind of treatment at this point in time," Hogan said. "There's a certain demographic with it, too, obviously, ones that are challenged."

Align Technology's stock (ALGN) plummeted 35.6% in midday trading, which puts it on track for the biggest one-day selloff since the record 38.3% drop on July 10, 2002. The stock, which was leading the S&P 500 index's SPX decliners, was also headed for the lowest close since April 27, 2017.

Hogan said that, based on case submissions by dentists, there continued to be strong interest in the Invisalign treatment. But weak conversion rates - people following through with orders - led to a lower-than-usual seasonal uptick in case starts, which historically has ticked up late in the quarter.

Another issue is that some dental practices - especially those with plenty of wires and metal brackets in stock and who haven't fully committed to investing in digital-scanning systems used to shape clear aligners due to financial uncertainty - are pushing the lower-cost metal brace option on people.

Given the current economic uncertainty and consumer hesitancy, the company is considering actions to cut costs, align its resources to match current demand and eliminate redundancies.

"First, we expect to realign certain business groups and reduce our global workforce," said Chief Financial Officer John Morici. "Second, we are looking to optimize our manufacturing footprint and dispose of certain manufacturing capital assets as we transition to next-generation manufacturing technologies, increase automation and regionalize manufacturing to be closer to our customers."

The company did not specify the number of layoffs, but said it would take one-time charges of $150 million to $170 million for the write-down of assets, restructuring charges and accelerated depreciation expenses, with $50 million to $60 million in charges expected to be recorded in the third quarter.

For Align's second quarter, the company said net income rose 29% from a year ago to $124.6 million, while adjusted earnings per share, which excludes nonrecurring items, of $2.49 missed the average analyst estimate compiled by FactSet of $2.57.

Revenue fell 1.6% to $1.01 billion, below the FactSet consensus of $1.06 billion.

Looking ahead, the company expects third-quarter revenue of $965 million to $985 million, below the current FactSet consensus of $986.7 million

Align Technology's stock has lost 37.1% in 2025, while the S&P 500 has gained 8.8%.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  07-31-25 1222ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
